title: Modern Styling with Utility-First CSS Frameworks

questions.sentence: As web development evolves the way we write CSS is also changing
Traditionally developers wrote custom CSS in separate files using class names like navbar or card
A modern alternative is using a UtilityFirst framework like Tailwind CSS
Instead of writing custom styles you use predefined utility classes directly in your HTML such as bgblue500 p4 or flex
This avoids the need to jump back and forth between HTML and CSS files making development much faster
Learning Tailwind CSS is now a popular requirement for many modern front end developer roles
